marn_intro.speaker: Marn
lines: This is not the pantry anymore, but I still check the shelves when the harbour rain starts counting.
Divorcee Harbour keeps old rooms under the floorboards. SRMT's weather makes every basement remember something wrong.
If Shrububu still wants KFC, she should follow whoever is most afraid of her appetite.

marn_after_battle.speaker: Marn
lines: The old practice echo is settled.
Now listen to the rain. It is spelling Satyaki, but the last letter keeps turning into a chicken leg.
When the harbour is free, do not trust the quiet. SRMT has a talent for sending silence ahead of him.

opening_not_kfc.speaker: Shrububu
lines: This sign promised crispy destiny.
This is not KFC.
The door made its choice, and the building followed.
Shrububu is disappointed enough to close the door normally next time, if the next door deserves it.

resident_satyaki_clue.speaker: Harbour Resident
lines: You broke the whole building with one disappointed door slam.
Poojan saw it. The sheriff wants to know if Ishiville finally has a chance.
If you are really Shrububu, follow the divorce records toward Satyaki before SRMT's collectors notice the noise.

poojan_intro.speaker: Sheriff Poojan
lines: Name is Poojan. I keep order in Divorcee Harbour, or what passes for order in SRMT weather.
That building was cursed, but you still folded it like wet cardboard.
Fight me once. If you stand, the revolver is yours, and the town gets to hope without whispering.

poojan_after.speaker: Sheriff Poojan
lines: All right, Shrububu. You are not a rumor.
Take the revolver. Satyaki Tirumal understands paperwork, not mercy.
The harbour women have waited long enough, and every gull here is carrying SRMT's bad news.

satyaki_intro.speaker: Satyaki Tirumal
lines: Shrububu. You took half my money, half my property, and somehow all the weather.
I turned this harbour into a filing cabinet because revenge requires documentation.
SRMT lets me keep this dock as long as I keep the town afraid. Sign here, then vanish.

satyaki_defeat.speaker: Satyaki Tirumal
lines: The deeds... the rings... the arguments...
Fine. Keep the harbour. Keep getting larger. SRMT will notice.
There is still no KFC here, which is the only victory I have left.

divorcee_lantern_woman.speaker: Lantern Woman
lines: Every porch lamp in Divorcee Harbour blinks twice when Satyaki lies.
Tonight they are blinking like a cheap motel sign.
Ask the docks about KFC if you want, but they only serve fog and custody paperwork.

divorcee_boathouse_woman.speaker: Boathouse Woman
lines: The tide brings legal envelopes in, and we throw them back before they hatch.
Satyaki thinks the harbour owes him sympathy.
I think Shrububu owes him a concise explanation delivered with a revolver.

divorcee_raincoat_woman.speaker: Raincoat Woman
lines: You came for KFC and found us instead.
That is how Ishiville works now: wrong doors, wet clues, and SRMT laughing through the storm drains.
If you grow any taller, please duck under my laundry line.

divorcee_radio_woman.speaker: Radio Woman
lines: My radio only plays static, weather reports, and Satyaki complaining about alimony.
Last night it coughed out the words 'Area 111' and then demanded chicken.
That felt like your kind of omen.

divorcee_dock_woman.speaker: Dock Woman
lines: After Satyaki falls, the dock stops creaking his name.
The first boat out is carrying everyone who wants a normal breakfast.
The second boat is for your KFC search, if it survives SRMT's fog.

clue_damp_divorce_records.speaker: Damp Divorce Records
lines: The pages list properties, grudges, and one furious note: 'Shrububu got the good ceiling fans.'
Satyaki stamped SRMT's sigil in the margin like a man proud of selling out to weather.

clue_fake_chicken_sign.speaker: Fake Chicken Sign
lines: The sign says 'Kentucky-Adjacent Feelings Cafe' in peeling yellow paint.
A tiny disclaimer says it has never served KFC, chicken, or emotional stability.

clue_sheriff_notice.speaker: Sheriff Notice
lines: Poojan's notice warns residents not to approach the east dock without proof.
Below it, someone wrote: 'Proof is a woman who can break a building by being disappointed.'

harbour_post_clear_lamps.speaker: Harbour Lamps
lines: After Satyaki's defeat, the lamps stop blinking warnings and start blinking menu items.
None of the menu items are KFC, but the harbour is trying.

harbour_post_clear_resident.speaker: Harbour Resident
lines: Divorcee Harbour is ours again.
Go inland, Shrububu. Banana-burbs is too happy, and nobody under SRMT should be that relaxed.

divorcee_boathouse_woman_post.speaker: Boathouse Woman
lines: The tide returned every legal envelope unopened.
Satyaki's dock key dissolved the moment he lost. Very theatrical metalwork.
Go find your chicken. We will turn his filing cabinet into a bait shop.

divorcee_raincoat_woman_post.speaker: Raincoat Woman
lines: The rain feels like rain again instead of surveillance.
You are taller. My laundry line has filed a formal complaint.
Banana-burbs is east. Their smiles arrive half a second before their faces.

divorcee_radio_woman_post.speaker: Radio Woman
lines: The static stopped repeating Satyaki's bank balance.
Now it says 'one-six-five' whenever the wind points inland.
SRMT does not like loose files. You should become a loose file.

divorcee_dock_woman_intro.speaker: Dock Woman
lines: Satyaki sealed the east dock with two locks: Poojan's doubt and his own paperwork.
Beat the first. Steal the second from the records office.
Then knock. He hates knocking. It sounds too much like consequences.
